affinity-proteomics.description: SDRF template for affinity-based proteomics experiments (Olink, SomaScan). This is the base template for all affinity proteomics experiments.

columns.name: source name
columns.description: Unique identifier for the biological sample

columns.name: assay name
columns.description: Unique identifier for the data acquisition run

columns.name: technology type
columns.description: Type of technology used

values: protein expression profiling by antibody array
protein expression profiling by aptamer array

columns.name: comment[technical replicate]
columns.description: Identifier for the technical replicate (integer starting from 1)

columns.name: comment[data file]
columns.description: Name of the raw data file

columns.name: comment[sdrf version]
columns.description: Version of the SDRF-Proteomics specification used to annotate this file

columns.name: comment[sdrf template]
columns.description: Template name and version used for annotation. Two formats are supported - key=value format (NT=template_name;VV=vX.Y.Z) or simple format (template_name vX.Y.Z). Multiple templates can be specified using multiple columns.

params.description: Template can be specified as 'NT=name;VV=vX.Y.Z' or 'name vX.Y.Z'

columns.name: comment[sdrf annotation tool]
columns.description: Software tool or method used to generate or annotate the SDRF file. Two formats are supported - key=value format (NT=tool_name;VV=vX.Y.Z) or simple format (tool_name vX.Y.Z).

params.description: Annotation tool can be specified as 'NT=name;VV=vX.Y.Z' or 'name vX.Y.Z' or 'manual curation'

columns.name: comment[sdrf validation hash]
columns.description: Hash value for SDRF validation integrity checking

columns.name: characteristics[organism]
columns.description: Species of the sample using NCBI Taxonomy

params.description: The organism should be a valid NCBI Taxonomy term

columns.name: characteristics[organism part]
columns.description: Main normalized anatomical term for the sample

params.description: The organism part should be a valid UBERON or BTO term

columns.name: characteristics[tissue supergroup]
columns.description: Broader anatomical grouping or system-level bucket for the sample, used alongside the organism part for higher-level grouping

params.description: Tissue supergroup should be a valid broad UBERON or BTO anatomy term when possible

columns.name: characteristics[cell type]
columns.description: Cell type of the sample

params.description: The cell type should be a valid Cell Ontology (CL), BRENDA Tissue Ontology (BTO), or Cell Line Ontology (CLO) term

columns.name: characteristics[biological replicate]
columns.description: Identifier for the biological replicate (integer starting from 1, or 'pooled' for pooled samples)

params.description: Biological replicate should be an integer or 'pooled' for pooled reference samples

columns.name: characteristics[pooled sample]
columns.description: Whether the sample is a pooled sample combining material from multiple biological sources. Use 'not pooled' for individual samples, 'pooled' when sources are unknown, or 'SN=sample1;SN=sample2' to list source names.

params.description: Use 'not pooled', 'pooled', or list sample IDs with SN= prefix

columns.name: characteristics[sample type]
columns.description: Role of the sample in the assay. Distinguishes study samples from controls/reference materials for assay interpretation and quality control

params.description: Sample type should be a child of PRIDE:0000895 (sample type)

columns.name: characteristics[disease]
columns.description: Disease state of the sample

params.description: The disease should be a valid MONDO, EFO, DOID, NCIT, or PATO ontology term. Use 'normal' (PATO:0000461) for healthy samples.

columns.name: characteristics[material type]
columns.description: Type of biological material being analyzed

columns.name: characteristics[tissue mass]
columns.description: Mass of tissue used for extraction

columns.name: characteristics[biosample accession number]
columns.description: BioSample accession number for the sample (e.g., SAMN or SAMEA identifiers)

columns.name: characteristics[sampling time]
columns.description: Time at which the sample was collected (for longitudinal or time-course studies)

columns.name: characteristics[treatment]
columns.description: Treatment or perturbation applied to the sample (drug, stimulus, environmental stress)

params.description: Treatment should be a valid ontology term. Use 'untreated' for controls.

columns.name: characteristics[synthetic peptide]
columns.description: Whether the sample is a synthetic peptide library or biological material

columns.name: characteristics[spiked compound]
columns.description: Spiked-in compound details using key-value format (CT=compound type, QY=quantity, PS=peptide sequence, AC=UniProt accession, CN=compound name, CV=vendor)

params.description: Key-value format for spiked compound details (CT=type, SP=species, QY=quantity, PS=sequence, AC=accession, CN=name, CV=vendor)

columns.name: characteristics[enrichment process]
columns.description: Enrichment strategy applied to the sample (e.g., phosphopeptide enrichment, crosslinked peptide enrichment, glycopeptide enrichment)

params.description: Enrichment type should be a child term of EFO:0009090 (enrichment process)

columns.name: comment[platform]
columns.description: Affinity proteomics platform used (e.g. Olink Explore HT, SomaScan Assay 7K)

params.description: The platform should be a valid PRIDE ontology term for affinity platforms

columns.name: comment[instrument]
columns.description: Instrument used for data acquisition (e.g. sequencer, qPCR machine, microarray reader)

params.description: The instrument should be a valid PSI-MS ontology term

columns.name: comment[panel name]
columns.description: Commercial panel/menu identifier within the platform. RECOMMENDED, but MUST be provided when not uniquely inferable from comment[platform].

columns.name: comment[quantification unit]
columns.description: Unit of quantification for the assay (platform-specific)

columns.name: comment[plate]
columns.description: Plate identifier for batch effect analysis

columns.name: comment[normalization method]
columns.description: Normalization method applied to quantification values

columns.name: comment[dilution]
columns.description: Sample dilution factor used (commonly used for SomaScan workflows)

columns.name: comment[lot number]
columns.description: Reagent lot number for traceability

metaproteomics.description: Base SDRF template for metaproteomics experiments (microbial community proteomics). Extends base directly and defines MIxS-aligned sample metadata. When combined with ms-proteomics, sample-metadata columns (organism, disease, cell type) are excluded. Use a child template (human-gut, soil, water) for environment-specific fields.

columns.name: characteristics[organism]
columns.description: Species of the sample using NCBI Taxonomy

params.description: The organism should be a valid NCBI Taxonomy term

columns.name: characteristics[organism part]
columns.description: Main normalized anatomical term for the sample

params.description: The organism part should be a valid UBERON or BTO term

columns.name: characteristics[tissue supergroup]
columns.description: Broader anatomical grouping or system-level bucket for the sample, used alongside the organism part for higher-level grouping

params.description: Tissue supergroup should be a valid broad UBERON or BTO anatomy term when possible

columns.name: characteristics[cell type]
columns.description: Cell type of the sample

params.description: The cell type should be a valid Cell Ontology (CL), BRENDA Tissue Ontology (BTO), or Cell Line Ontology (CLO) term

columns.name: characteristics[biological replicate]
columns.description: Identifier for the biological replicate (integer starting from 1, or 'pooled' for pooled samples)

params.description: Biological replicate should be an integer or 'pooled' for pooled reference samples

columns.name: characteristics[pooled sample]
columns.description: Whether the sample is a pooled sample combining material from multiple biological sources. Use 'not pooled' for individual samples, 'pooled' when sources are unknown, or 'SN=sample1;SN=sample2' to list source names.

params.description: Use 'not pooled', 'pooled', or list sample IDs with SN= prefix

columns.name: characteristics[sample type]
columns.description: Classification of the sample role in the experiment. Distinguishes experimental samples from controls, references, and other roles in multiplexed or plate-based experiments.

params.description: Sample type should be a child of PRIDE:0000895 (sample type)

columns.name: characteristics[disease]
columns.description: Disease state of the sample

params.description: The disease should be a valid MONDO, EFO, DOID, NCIT, or PATO ontology term. Use 'normal' (PATO:0000461) for healthy samples.

columns.name: characteristics[material type]
columns.description: Type of biological material being analyzed

columns.name: characteristics[tissue mass]
columns.description: Mass of tissue used for extraction

columns.name: characteristics[biosample accession number]
columns.description: BioSample accession number for the sample (e.g., SAMN or SAMEA identifiers)

columns.name: characteristics[sampling time]
columns.description: Time at which the sample was collected (for longitudinal or time-course studies)

columns.name: characteristics[treatment]
columns.description: Treatment or perturbation applied to the sample (drug, stimulus, environmental stress)

params.description: Treatment should be a valid ontology term. Use 'untreated' for controls.

columns.name: characteristics[synthetic peptide]
columns.description: Whether the sample is a synthetic peptide library or biological material

columns.name: characteristics[spiked compound]
columns.description: Spiked-in compound details using key-value format (CT=compound type, QY=quantity, PS=peptide sequence, AC=UniProt accession, CN=compound name, CV=vendor)

params.description: Key-value format for spiked compound details (CT=type, SP=species, QY=quantity, PS=sequence, AC=accession, CN=name, CV=vendor)

columns.name: characteristics[enrichment process]
columns.description: Enrichment strategy applied to the sample (e.g., phosphopeptide enrichment, crosslinked peptide enrichment, glycopeptide enrichment)

params.description: Enrichment type should be a child term of EFO:0009090 (enrichment process)

columns.name: comment[instrument]
columns.description: Mass spectrometer instrument used

params.description: The instrument should be a valid PSI-MS or PRIDE ontology term

columns.name: comment[ion source]
columns.description: Ion source used for ionization (e.g. ESI, APCI, EI, MALDI). Should be a child of MS:1000008.

params.description: Ion source should be a child term of MS:1000008

columns.name: comment[scan polarity]
columns.description: Scan polarity (positive scan, negative scan, or polarity switching). Polarity switching is reserved for true in-method switching producing a single file; otherwise use two rows per sample (one per polarity).

params.description: Scan polarity must be one of positive scan, negative scan, polarity switching

columns.name: comment[acquisition method]
columns.description: Mass spectrometry acquisition method (DDA, DIA, SIM, MRM, full scan)

params.description: Acquisition method should be a valid PRIDE ontology term

columns.name: comment[metabolite assignment file]
columns.description: Pointer to the metabolite assignment file (MAF) for this assay. Per-assay constant — the same value is repeated across all rows of the same assay. Mirrors the MetaboBank semantics of Comment[Metabolite Assignment File].

params.description: Path or filename of the MAF file

columns.name: comment[extraction method]
columns.description: Sample extraction method used to obtain the metabolite fraction

params.description: Free-text extraction method, optionally CHMO-style NT=...;AC=CHMO:NNNNNNN

columns.name: comment[extraction solvent]
columns.description: Solvent or solvent mixture used for metabolite extraction

params.description: Extraction solvent should be a valid ChEBI term when possible

columns.name: characteristics[analyte class]
columns.description: Class of metabolites targeted by the extraction and acquisition method

params.description: Analyte class describes the metabolite fraction targeted

columns.name: comment[internal standard]
columns.description: Internal standard(s) spiked into the sample for normalization or retention-time calibration

params.description: Internal standard should be a valid ChEBI term when possible

columns.name: characteristics[sample matrix]
columns.description: Type of biological matrix used as input (e.g. serum, plasma, CSF, urine, tissue extract)

params.description: Sample matrix should be a valid UBERON or BTO term

columns.name: comment[sample preparation batch]
columns.description: Batch identifier for sample preparation (plate, chip, processing batch). Useful for batch-effect correction.

columns.name: comment[lc batch]
columns.description: Liquid chromatography batch identifier for batch-effect tracking (e.g. column changes, LC system swaps)

columns.name: comment[acquisition date]
columns.description: Date of MS data acquisition (ISO 8601 format recommended). Useful for tracking instrument drift and batch effects.

columns.name: comment[ms2 mass analyzer]
columns.description: Mass analyzer used for MS2 acquisition

params.description: Should be a valid PSI-MS ontology term

columns.description: MS method-defined minimum precursor (MS1) m/z setting used to acquire the data

columns.description: MS method-defined maximum precursor (MS1) m/z setting used to acquire the data

columns.description: m/z scan range for MS2 spectra as an interval. Alternative to separate ms2 min mz / ms2 max mz columns.
